characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A Chicago-based criminal-defense lawyer who is quirky and razor-sharp in equal measure. In this series, she moves to New York to oversee a consent decree against the NYPD and ensure that the cops operate within the scope of the law and don't lock up innocent people in the name of case closure. Or so most are led to believe. In reality, the consent decree is cover for her to investigate the precinct's captain, C.W. Wagner, for corruption. For tropes related to her onThe Good Wife, clickhere. Amazingly Embarrassing Parents: Elsbeth absolutelydotes onTeddy, but she's also very protective of him, and goes through a very long conversation with Roy, Teddy's boyfriend, for almost 90 minutes. Amoral Attorney: Some of her less-than-honorable actions from her days as a lawyer come back to haunt her in season 2.

characters.name: Elsbeth
characters.description: A Chicago-based criminal-defense lawyer who is quirky and razor-sharp in equal measure. In this series, she moves to New York to oversee a consent decree against the NYPD and ensure that the cops operate within the scope of the law and don't lock up innocent people in the name of case closure. Or so most are led to believe. In reality, the consent decree is cover for her to investigate the precinct's captain, C.W. Wagner, for corruption. For tropes related to her onThe Good Wife, clickhere. Amazingly Embarrassing Parents: Elsbeth absolutelydotes onTeddy, but she's also very protective of him, and goes through a very long conversation with Roy, Teddy's boyfriend, for almost 90 minutes. Amoral Attorney: Some of her less-than-honorable actions from her days as a lawyer come back to haunt her in season 2.

characters.name: Officer (later Det.) Blanke
characters.description: A beat cop in the precinct who quickly takes a shine to Elsbeth. Conflicting Loyalty: In the first season, Kaya is caught in the middle when she finds out Elsbeth is investigating Wagner for potential corruption. Kaya has worked for Wagner for years, respects him greatly, and swears she's never seen him behave in anything except an honorable manner, but she also trusts Elsbeth and knows she wouldn't be poking into this for no reason. To make it more complicated, Wagner is aware Elsbeth is looking into him and has asked Kaya to keep tabs on her for him, while Elsbeth admits she isn't sure Wagner is guilty or not. Curly Hair Is Ugly: Played with. Kaya has long, curly hair, and goes through anExpository Hairstyle Changewhen she gets promoted to detective, having her hair straightened. The implication is that she straightened it to look more "professional." "Friends" Rent Control: While Elsbeth is established as having made a lot of money in Chicago, Kaya is a cop. She's shown to live in a decently-sized house in Queens by herself after she stops rooming with Elsbeth. Put on a Bus:By the end of Season 2, she has taken position in a task force in Washington D.C.

characters.name: Captain Wagner
characters.description: The captain of the precinct, whom Elsbeth is investigating for corruption. Actor Allusion: Season 3 has him playing a trombone, like his character inSeries/Treme. Black and Nerdy: Season 2 reveals that he's a huge space geek. Dirty Cop: Elsbeth is placed within the precinct to see if he is taking bribes from the sweatshop making his wife's clothing and putting a kibosh into any investigation of it. It turns out he is innocent, and Lt. Noonan has been framing him. Do Not Call Me "Paul": Wagner doesn't like being called by his given name, "Charles Wallace", and gets rankled when Celetano dares to say it aloud even if it's in the privacy of Wagner's office. He's also not happy when Elsbeth blurts it out in the bullpen.

characters.name: Lt. Noonan
characters.description: Angry White Man: Noonan believes thatPolitical Correctness Is Eviland that Wagner was promoted above him because of it rather than because ofmerit. Big Bad Friend: Noonan is a close friend to Captain Wagner, who he was also throwing under the bus for his own crimes. Bigot with a Badge: Noonan is aDirty Copwho believes Wagner (apparently a close friend of his) was promoted due to political correctness. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: He is a close friend to Captain Wagner, and yet is throwing Wagner under the bus for his own crimes. Broken Pedestal: Wagner considered Noonan to be family, even making Noonan godfather to his children. He's devastated to find out that Noonan was deeply corrupt and setting him up to take the fall.

characters.name: Lt. Connor
characters.description: A lieutenant brought in to replace Dave Noonan and help improve the department's efficiency. Affirmative-Action Legacy: Ironically, given that Noonan's issue is thatPolitical Correctness Is Evil, his replacement is of Asian descent. Asian and Nerdy: He introduces himself as a numbers guy who considersMoneyballhis favorite movie. He also dresses up asWinston Churchillfor Halloween. The Bore: Connor isThe Stoic, inexpressive, and fond of numbers. However, he does eventually warm up to Elsbeth. Everyone Has Standards: Connor may be an introvert who loves technology, but in "Bunker Down" he gives a"The Reason You Suck" Speechto introverted tech guru Craig Harris when he realizes the man is a violent, paranoid misanthrope.

characters.name: Det. Smullen
characters.description: An old-school detective in the department who tends to butt heads with Elsbeth. Inspector Lestrade: He's a competent detective trusted by Captain Wagner. However, he's used to solving straightforward cases and is slow to pick up the plot when confronted with murderers who use elaborate ruses to try and get the police off their scents.

characters.name: Det. Browner
characters.description: A rather aggressive detective who's focused on closing his cases quickly using the most-obvious solutions he sees. Inspector Lestrade: Once he has his sights on a suspect, he's quite dogged in taking that person down. Whenever Elsbeth starts picking his cases apart, he digs his heels and tries harder to reinforce his position. Put on a Bus: As part of Judge Crawford's attempts to get Elsbeth to back off, he gets reassigned to another precinct as part of the layoffs Crawford arranges. Sequel Non-Entity: Each of the detectives Elsbeth works with appears multiple times except Browner, who only appears in "A Classic New York Character" and then isn't seen again. It would beChuck Cunningham Syndromeexcept that his absence isHand Wavedlate in Season 2 (a whole season and a half since his one and only appearance) by saying he got reassigned as part of Crawford's layoffs.

characters.name: Det. Donnelly
characters.description: A detective in the NYPD that often butts heads with Elsbeth. Inspector Lestrade: She thinks she's going after run-of-the-mill murders and not carefully premeditated schemes so she isn't as quick as Elsbeth in spotting the loose threads murderers leave dangling. Not So Above It All: She’s giddy with glee at the mere opportunity to bust a jewelry heist. Pet the Dog:While she had no reason to, she comes to Elsbeth’s fashion show debut to support her in“A Fitting Finale”.In“Elsbeth Flips the Bird”, she (alongs with Buzz and Edwards) happily play poker with Elsbeth despite them usually finding her annoying, showing that off the clock, they can all be friends.In"Ick, A Bod", she encourages Elsbeth to be optimistic that even though she doesn't know her neighbors, they are still looking out for her. While she had no reason to, she comes to Elsbeth’s fashion show debut to support her in“A Fitting Finale”.

characters.name: Det. Edwards
characters.description: An NYPD detective who likes to use the latest technologies in pursuing her cases. Broken Pedestal: She finds herself disappointed to learn that Quinn Powers is a fraud and Cerberus is a scam. Hidden Depths:"Hot Tub Crime Machine"reveals that she is in a polyamorous relationship. Ludd Was Right: Edwards thinks that technology is the way forward in policing and criminal investigations. However, Elsbeth always ends up figuring out the truth using old-fashioned legwork and insightful questioning. Polyamory: As stated above, she is in one such relationship, though she doesn't elaborate much further on it.

characters.name: Det. Fleming
characters.description: An older detective who takes Kaya under his wing as she works towards the detective department. I Coulda Been a Contender!: Fleming says that his life was completely changed and "the wind was taken out of his sails" by the corruption in the Moresco case. A Day in the Limelight: While he appears a lot,"Scenes from an Italian Restaurant"focuses onThat One Casefor him and gives him significantly more character focus. That One Case: For him, it's his very first case as a homicide detective, the murder of Goldie Moresco. He always figured there was more to it than the Eddie Nova angle, but the department was happy to brush the whole thing off as gang violence.

characters.name: Det. Rivers
characters.description: One of the detectives who handled the investigation into Andy Mertens' murder. As an exchange of favors for the captain of his precinct reopening the investigation, Elsbeth and her precinct have to take him on, which proves to be quite the struggle for them all. Character Development: While he was introduced as aHate Sink, by "I Know What You Did Thirty-Three Summers Ago" he's showing more of an interest in justice and willingness to learn from his colleagues. Clueless Detective: Rivers is screamingly bad as an investigator, always jumping to flimsy conclusions to arrest suspects and working off of shaky evidence on a good day. Even the Loving Hero Has Hated Ones: Due to how much of an unprofessional, rude, and sexist man Detective Rivers is, he is one of the few people that Elsbeth genuinely dislikes, which surprises even herself when she realizes it. She even recommends to Captain Wagner that Rivers get a reprimand and that it go in his permanent file. Freudian Excuse: "Otherwise Enraged" reveals that he wanted to become a cop after his aunt was killed in a hit-and-run when he was twelve.

characters.name: Officer Reese Chandler
characters.description: A beat cop assigned to help Elsbeth Tascioni with her work after Kaya becomes detective. Motor Mouth: He's very chatty at first even for the chatty Elsbeth mentioning among others that he doesCross Fitand that his nana had a glass eye which used to freak him out. The Watson: In his debut episode he shadows Elsbeth and asks questions that help the audience better understand Elsbeth's thought processes.

characters.name: Officer Hackett
characters.description: Cope by Creating: She works through her ideas by crafting, and uses stand-up comedy as her outlet away from being a cop. Insult Comic: Has some traces of this in her act, making some light jokes at the expense of Wagner and Fleming when they show up to her show. Married to the Job: While she likes doing stand up as a release, she is dedicated to her career as a cop.

characters.name: Det. Taylor
characters.description: Ascended Fangirl: Her first case in "Basket Case" investigates the St. Ivan's basketball team and their legendary coach, Coach Willoughby, both of whom she is a fan of. However, she keeps it professional. Played Ball in College: Taylor herself played women's basketball in college, so she knows the ins and outs of the sport when assigned to the St. Ivan's case. Tomboy and Girly Girl: When paired with Elsbeth, especially since their first case involved college basketball (which Nina is well-versed in and Elsbeth is less familiar with) and their second involved a debutante ball (which Elsbeth gets into while Nina criticizes).

characters.name: Teddy
characters.description: Elsbeth's adult son who has decided to move closer to his mom in New York. Alliterative Name:TeddyTascioni. Fatal Attractor: Elsbeth mentions in"Something Blue"that, based on his last few boyfriends, Teddy doesn't really seem to fall for kind or honest people. His new boyfriend Roy in Season 2 is a break from this trend. Following in Relative's Footsteps:Zig-Zagged. Teddy starts out wanting nothing to do with the law since both his parents were lawyers, were rarely around, and, in his view, were supporting a corrupt system. He starts to soften on this stance seeing his mother use her law background to really help people, taking the LSAT and looking into law schools.Judge Crawford's manipulation of the legal system and death as a martyr turns Teddy against it again, but using the same attention to detail as his mother to find evidence against Judge Dousant in the Season 2 finale implied he would play it straight in Season 3. Downplayed in Season 3, as Teddy settles on becoming a journalist, exposing truth by holding those in power accountable, not unlike what his mother does. Unseen No More: ThroughoutThe Good Wifeand Season 1, Teddy was only ever mentioned by Elsbeth in conversation asThe Ghost, but finally appears in person in Season 2's "Gold, Frankincense, and Murder".

characters.name: Claudia
characters.description: Captain Wagner's wife. Nobody Suspects the Woman: Invoked in"I Know What You Did Thirty-Three Summers Ago"when Claudia steals a glass from Milton Crawford, saying he wouldn't know what she was coming.

characters.name: Julia
characters.description: Captain Wagner's eldest daughter. Dating What Daddy Hates: While her first date pick was only objectionable because the man in question was in a relationship, her second choice isDetective Rivers, aHe-Man Woman Haterwidely disliked by Wagner and his entire precinct. She later dumps Rivers because of his constant need for Wagner's approval. Gadgeteer Genius: She comes in to help the precinct (and her father) with tech stuff. Jerkass Ball: In "Otherwise Enraged", Julia gives her father an ultimatum to uninvite her ex, Det. Rivers, from their anniversary party or she won't come, because she was angry her father never ran the idea by her before inviting him. She comes around by the end and realizes she should have talked to Rivers directly and not let her discomfort potentially ruin her parents' celebration or hurt Rivers for no good reason. Infantilization Retaliation: Julia is frustrated that her father still sees her as a little girl when she's 27 years old, and feels like he babies her more than his other children.

characters.name: Agent Celetano
characters.description: A federal agent who is investigating Captain Wagner for corruption. Inspector Javert: He is determined to root out any potential corruption involving Wagner and is unmoved by Elsbeth's pleas to ease up based on her gut instincts.

characters.name: Judge Crawford
characters.description: Arc Villain: In the second season, with him initially getting away with his murder of Andy and causing problems for Elsbeth when she keeps digging into him. Asshole Victim: The man murdered a girl for not wanting to be with him, murdered Andy when having him alive proved too risky for his career, used his connections to sabotage Elsbeth and the people she cared about, and effectively ruined Delia Kirby's life, what with trying to frame her and even destroying her reputation even when she got acquitted. Being locked up for his crimes would have been best, but he certainly worked to earn that bullet. Big Bad: From his appearance in "One Angry Woman", Crawford becomes a villain that looms large over Season 2. Even when he isn't shown onscreen, he's disrupting theNYPDrunning andafter his death, his ally sends Elsbeth to prison. Blue Blood: Crawford is extremely proud of his blue-blooded lineage and is fond of aClass-Based Insultto anyone he views as impeding him. Casting Gag:Michael Emerson(alongside beingCarrie Preston's husband) previously played Leland Townsend, a soft-spoken and evilPsycho Psychologistknown for going up against the main character in the Kings' previous TV series,Evil (2019). Here, he plays an evil judge who once again uses the law to spread misery and get away with his own crimes.

characters.name: Angus
characters.description: A Scottish musician/fireman who witnesses a New York murder from his small village via The Iris interactive installation in "Tiny Town". Accent Interest: According toWord of God, part of Elsbeth's attraction to Angus is his accent isn't one she hears all the time. Firemen Are Hot: Played with. The end of "Tiny Town" reveals that his day job is firefighting, and he is Elsbeth's to-date onlyLove Interest. However, it bothers her as much as she's attracted to him, because Elsbeth knows that firemen are always attracted to her becauseof her red hair. Girls Like Musicians: Elsbeth is charmed by Angus's music. Long-Distance Relationship: Elsbeth and Angus meet via virtual portal, falling in love an ocean apart. They continue to see each other in spite of staying in their respective countries.

characters.name: Alec Bloom
characters.description: Broken Pedestal:When Elsbeth is told there is no evidence Alec was ever homeless as a child, as he states in his campaign platform, her growing admiration of him is dampened. Doppelgänger Replacement Love Interest: Soon after breaking up with Angus, Elsbeth meets Alec at a benefit and they hit it off. Both love interests' names start with an A, they are bothTall, Dark, and Handsome, and they were both firemen at one time. Firemen Are Hot: Downplayed, as Alec is a former firefighter, but he isTall, Dark, and Handsome. No Celebrities Were Harmed: His political platform at least is inspired by that of NYC mayoral elect Zohran Mondani (though he wasn't yet an elect when the episodes were written). Photo Op with the Dog: They seem to be manufactured by Melissa more than by Alec himself, but she certainly takes every opportunity to photograph him doing good deeds, from charity fundraisers to convent food drives.

characters.name: Winnie Crawford
characters.description: Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: She seems to want to be friendly with Elsbeth in their first couple of meetings, but the audience sees she is using Elsbeth to get something she wants. Crusading Widow: Winnie is introduced using her husband's good name to support her fundraising efforts. Destroy the Evidence:When Elsbeth tips her off about a piece of evidence that went missing, Winnie goes home, looks through her husband's things, and burns the evidence.
